title: podman stop: spurious "failed to kill all processes / cgroup not exist" warning on rootless containers with systemd cgroup manager (v5.7.0) in
body:
### Issue Description

When running `podman stop` on a rootless container managed by the systemd cgroup manager, a spurious warning is emitted even though the container stops successfully and the cgroup lifecycle appears correct.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

1. podman start container
2. podman stop container


### Describe the results you received

WARN[0000] failed to kill all processes, possibly due to lack of cgroup
           (Hint: enable cgroup v2 delegation)  error="cgroup not exist"
mycontainer

### Describe the results you expected

No warning should be emitted

### podman info output

```yaml
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.42.1
  cgroupControllers:
  - cpuset
  - cpu
  - memory
  - pids
  cgroupManager: systemd
  cgroupVersion: v2
  conmon:
    package: conmon_2.1.13+ds1-2_amd64
    path: /usr/bin/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.1.13, commit: unknown'

      package: aardvark-dns_1.16.0-3_amd64
      path: /usr/lib/podman/aardvark-dns
      version: aardvark-dns 1.16.0
    package: netavark_1.16.1-3.1_amd64
    path: /usr/lib/podman/netavark
    version: netavark 1.16.1
  ociRuntime:
    name: crun
    package: crun_1.21-1ubuntu3_amd64
    path: /usr/bin/crun
    version: |-
      crun version 1.21
      commit: 10269840aa07fb7e6b7e1acff6198692d8ff5c88
      rundir: /run/user/1000/crun
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +CRIU +WASM:wasmedge +YAJL
  os: linux
  pasta:
    executable: /usr/bin/pasta
    package: passt_0.0~git20260120.386b5f5-1_amd64
    version: |
      pasta 0.0~git20260120.386b5f5-1

        <https://www.gnu.org/licenses/old-licenses/gpl-2.0.html>

      There is NO WARRANTY, to the extent permitted by law.
  remoteSocket:
    exists: true
    path: /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock
  rootlessNetworkCmd: pasta
  security:
    apparmorEnabled: false
    capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT
    rootless: true
    seccompEnabled: true
    seccompProfilePath: /usr/share/containers/seccomp.json
    selinuxEnabled: false
  serviceIsRemote: false
  slirp4netns:
    executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns
    package: slirp4netns_1.3.3-1_amd64
    version: |-

      libseccomp: 2.6.0
  swapFree: 8589930496
  swapTotal: 8589930496
  uptime: 1h 3m 19.00s (Approximately 0.04 days)
  variant: ""

  authorization: null
  log:
  - k8s-file
  - none
  - passthrough

  - macvlan

  containerStore:
    number: 2
    paused: 0
    running: 0
    stopped: 2
  graphDriverName: overlay
  graphOptions: {}
  graphRoot: /home/lorenzo-arecco/.local/share/containers/storage
  graphRootAllocated: 1003736440832
  graphRootUsed: 392135131136
  graphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Supports shifting: "false"
    Supports volatile: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  imageCopyTmpDir: /var/tmp
  imageStore:
    number: 1
  runRoot: /run/user/1000/containers
  transientStore: false
  volumePath: /home/lorenzo-arecco/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes

  APIVersion: 5.7.0
  BuildOrigin: Ubuntu
  Built: 1771860932

  GitCommit: ""
  GoVersion: go1.25.0
  Os: linux
  OsArch: linux/amd64
  Version: 5.7.0
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Environment

- **Podman version**: 5.7.0
- **OS**: Ubuntu 26.04 (host)
- **Container image**: docker.io/library/ubuntu:24.04
- **Cgroup version**: v2 (confirmed via `/proc/filesystems`)
- **Cgroup manager**: systemd
- **Runtime**: crun 1.21
- **Rootless**: yes
- **Linger**: enabled (`loginctl enable-linger`)
- **podman.socket**: enabled and active (`systemctl --user enable --now podman.socket`)

### Additional information

**cgroup v2 delegation is properly configured.**
The user slice correctly exposes all controllers:
```
$ cat /sys/fs/cgroup/user.slice/user-1000.slice/user@1000.service/cgroup.controllers
cpuset cpu memory pids
```

**The libpod systemd scope IS created while the container is running:**
```
$ systemctl --user list-units --type=scope | grep libpod
libpod-e4c301dadd763...scope  loaded active running  libcontainer container e4c301...
```

**`podman inspect` while the container is running shows a valid CgroupPath but an empty `Cgroup` field:**
```json
"CgroupPath": "/user.slice/user-1000.slice/user@1000.service/user.slice/libpod-.scope",
"CgroupManager": "systemd",
"CgroupMode": "private",
"Cgroup": "",
"CgroupParent": "user.slice"
```

The empty `"Cgroup": ""` field may be relevant — it suggests podman is not tracking the actual cgroup path internally, even though the systemd scope exists.

**Enabling `podman.socket` did not resolve the warning.**
The socket was inactive by default; enabling it (`systemctl --user enable --now podman.socket`) had no effect on the warning.

comments.body:
> podman start container
podman stop container

Things like that are no reproducer, they do not help. You need to provide exact commands, starting with how the container is actually created.

In particular your podman info says the default oci runtime is crun but the error you show is clearly something runc produces, see https://github.com/opencontainers/runc/issues/4732

So this is not something we control in podman.

> > podman start container
> podman stop container
> 
> Things like that are no reproducer, they do not help. You need to provide exact commands, starting with how the container is actually created.
> 
> In particular your podman info says the default oci runtime is crun but the error you show is clearly something runc produces, see https://github.com/opencontainers/runc/issues/4732
> 
> So this is not something we control in podman.

The container was created via distrobox. Could this be helpful?
